beginner.question: What does flex-grow do in Flexbox?
beginner.answer: flex-grow determines how much a flex item can grow relative to other items when there is extra space in the container.

beginner.question: What is the default value of flex-grow?
beginner.answer: The default value of flex-grow is 0, meaning items do not grow by default.

beginner.question: What does flex-grow: 1 do?
beginner.answer: flex-grow: 1 allows an item to grow to fill available space, sharing it equally with other items that also have flex-grow: 1.

beginner.question: What happens when all items have flex-grow: 1?
beginner.answer: All items grow equally to fill the container, each receiving an equal share of the available space.

beginner.question: What happens when one item has flex-grow: 2 and others have flex-grow: 1?
beginner.answer: The item with flex-grow: 2 receives twice as much of the extra space as each item with flex-grow: 1.

beginner.question: Does flex-grow affect the item's size beyond the available free space?
beginner.answer: No, flex-grow only distributes the available free space; items do not shrink to accommodate growing items.

beginner.question: Can flex-grow be a decimal value?
beginner.answer: Yes, flex-grow accepts positive numbers including decimals like 0.5.

beginner.question: What is the difference between flex-grow: 0 and flex-grow: 1?
beginner.answer: flex-grow: 0 prevents growth; flex-grow: 1 allows the item to expand to fill available space.

beginner.question: Does flex-grow work on the cross axis?
beginner.answer: No, flex-grow only affects the main axis size of flex items.

beginner.question: How is flex-grow used to create equal-width columns?
beginner.answer: Set flex-grow: 1 on all columns so each receives an equal share of the available space.

intermediate.question: How does flex-grow interact with flex-basis?
intermediate.answer: flex-grow distributes available space after subtracting the flex-basis values; items grow from their flex-basis size.

intermediate.question: How is the available space calculated for flex-grow distribution?
intermediate.answer: Available space = container size - sum of flex-basis values - gaps. This remaining space is distributed proportionally by flex-grow.

intermediate.question: What happens when flex-grow is applied with flex-basis: 0?
intermediate.answer: With flex-basis: 0, all space is distributed proportionally; items effectively share the container space based on grow factors.

intermediate.question: How do you make one flex item fill remaining space while others stay at their natural size?
intermediate.answer: Set flex-grow: 1 on the item you want to grow and flex-grow: 0 on the others.

intermediate.question: How does flex-grow work with max-width constraints?
intermediate.answer: An item cannot grow beyond its max-width, even if the grow factor would allow more space; leftover space goes to other items.

intermediate.question: How does flex-grow interact with margins like margin-left: auto?
intermediate.answer: margin: auto absorbs free space and takes priority over flex-grow, preventing growth.

intermediate.question: How does flex-grow behave in a column direction?
intermediate.answer: In column direction, flex-grow distributes vertical space instead of horizontal.

intermediate.question: What happens when all items have different flex-grow values in a single line?
intermediate.answer: Each item grows proportionally to its flex-grow value relative to the sum of all grow values.

intermediate.question: How does flex-grow interact with the wrap behavior?
intermediate.answer: flex-grow only distributes space within each flex line; items on different lines do not share space.

intermediate.question: How do you prevent a flex item from growing?
intermediate.answer: Set flex-grow: 0 on that item to prevent it from growing beyond its flex-basis.

advanced.question: How does the flex-grow algorithm work mathematically?
advanced.answer: Each item's growth = (grow factor / sum of all grow factors) * available free space, added to its flex-basis.

advanced.question: How does flex-grow interact with min-content and max-content sizing?
advanced.answer: min-content sets a floor and max-content a ceiling for the item; flex-grow cannot shrink below min-content or expand beyond max-content.

advanced.question: How does flex-grow behave with absolutely positioned items?
advanced.answer: Absolutely positioned children are not flex items and are completely ignored by flex-grow calculations.

advanced.question: How does flex-grow interact with the gap property when distributing space?
advanced.answer: Gaps are subtracted from the container space before flex-grow calculations, so they do not affect proportional distribution.

advanced.question: How does flex-grow work in a multi-line container when lines have different numbers of items?
advanced.answer: Each line independently distributes its available space; a line with fewer items allocates more space per item.

advanced.question: What happens when flex-grow is set on items inside an inline-flex container?
advanced.answer: flex-grow works the same way in inline-flex; items grow based on available space within the inline-level container.

advanced.question: How does flex-grow handle negative available space (overflow scenario)?
advanced.answer: flex-grow only adds space; it does not handle negative space. flex-shrink handles cases where items overflow.

advanced.question: How does the flex shorthand relate to flex-grow?
advanced.answer: The first value in the flex shorthand is flex-grow. flex: 1 sets flex-grow: 1, flex-shrink: 1, flex-basis: 0.

advanced.question: How does flex-grow affect the accessibility of content order?
advanced.answer: flex-grow does not change DOM order, only visual size, so it does not affect accessibility tree order.

advanced.question: How does flex-grow interact with aspect-ratio on flex items?
advanced.answer: When both flex-grow and aspect-ratio are set, the item grows on the main axis and the cross axis adjusts to maintain the ratio.
